sutta: once the exalted 1 was dwelling among the koreans at the market town called kakarapatta their long knee the korean clansmen visited the exalted 1 and after greeting him sat down at 1 side so seated he spoke thus to the exhorted 1 lord we householders are immersed in the realm of pleasure we are cumbered with dead meat and suns we delight in the muslin cloth from banares and in sandalwood we deck ourselves with flowers with garlands and cosmetics enjoy the use of both silver and gold that is money lord to such as us let the exalted 1 also teach dhamma teach the things which will be to our advantage and for our happiness here on earth for our advantage and happiness in the world to come and the buddha said these 4 conditions tiger foot lead to a clansman's advantage and happiness here on earth what for achievement in alertness achievement and weariness good company and the even life and what tiger food is achievement in alertness let's stop here a moment this tiger footnote must be the nickname of this person this long name is bian his his family name was bianga padja which is tiger foot and longnee is was his nickname so here the buddha addresses him by his family name and what tiger fruit is achievement in alertness herein by whatsoever activity a clansmen make is living whether by the plow by trading or by cattle herding by archery or as a raja's man or by any of the crops he is depth and tireless gifted with an inquiring turn of mind into ways and means he is able to arrange and carry out his job this is called achievement in alertness and what tiger food is achievement in weariness hearing whatsoever wealth a clansmen get together by work and zeal collect by the strength of his arm earned by the sweat of his brow and just be obtained in a lawful manner such he keeps by watching war thinking now how can i arrange so that rajas may not get this wealth out of me nor thieves steal it no fire consuming no water carried off nor treacherous airs make off with it this is called achievement in wariness and what tiger food is good company here in whatsoever a village or market town a clansman dwell he consorts converses engages in talk with householders or householders sons young men reared in virtue old men old in virtue full of faith virtue charity and wisdom he emulates the fullness of faith in such as are full of faith he emulates the fullness of virtue in such as are full of virtue he emulates the fullness of charity in such as are full of charity he emulates the fullness of wisdom in such as are full of wisdom this is called good company and what tiger food is the even life hearing a clansman while experiencing both gain and loss in wealth continues his business serenely not unduly elated or depressed he thinks thus my income after deducting the loss will stand at so much and my outgoings will not exceed my income this is 1 who carries scales or his apprentice knows on holding up the balance that either by so much it has dipped down or by so much it has tilted up even so tiger foot a clansman experiencing both gain and loss continues his business serenely neither unduly elated nor unduly depressed but realizes that after allowing for the loss his income will stand at so much and that his outgoings will not exceed his income if tiger food this clansmen have but small earnings and live on a grand scale he will be rumored of him this clansman eats his wealth like a victory glutton and if his earnings be great and he lives meanly rumor will say of him this plantsman will die like a starvaling therefore this glandsman continues his business serenely knowing that his outgoings will not exceed his income this is called the even life tiger food the 4 channels for the flowing away of a mass wealth are these looseness with women debauchery in drinking navery in dice play and friendship companionship and intimacy with evil doers such just as in the case of a great reservoir with only 4 inlets and only 4 outlets if a man should close the inlets and open the outlets and there should be no proper fall of rain a lessening is to be expected in that great reservoir and not an increase even so tiger foot there are these 4 channels for the flowing away of a mass wealth looseness with women debauchery and drinking navery and dice play and friendship companionship and intimacy with evil doers the 4 channels for the flowing in of great wealth are these abstinence from looseness with women from debauchery in drinking from knavery in dice play and having friendship companionship and intimacy with the good just as in the case of a great reservoir with only 4 inlets and only 4 outlets if the man should open the inlets and close the outlets and if there should be a proper fall of rain an increase may be expected in that great reservoir and not a lessening even so tiger foot there are these 4 channels for the flowing in of great wealth abstinence from looseness with women from debauchery and drinking from navery and dice play and the friendship companionship and intimacy with a group these tiger food are the 4 conditions which lead to a cleansement advantage and happiness here on earth these 4 conditions tiger food lead to a cleansements advantage and happiness in the world to come what for achievement in faith achievement and virtue achievement in charity and achievement in wisdom and what is achievement in faith hearing a clansman has faith and believes in the awakening of the tata gather thinking of a truth he is the exalted 1 this is called achievement in faith and what is achievement in virtue hearing the cleansement abstains from taking life from stealing from adultery from lying and from taking liquor this is called achievement in virtue and what is achievement in charity here in a clansman dwells at home with heart perish of the stain of everest and etc etc this is called achievement in charity and what is achievement and wisdom hearing a clansman is wise and is endowed with wisdom into the rise and fall of things etc this is called achievement and wisdom these tiger foot are the 4 conditions which lead to a cleanse means advantage and happiness in the world to come that's the end of the sutta
commentary: so this suta is a very practical suta for lay persons and because this person who came to ask the buddha he said he still wants to enjoy life as a lay person but what are the things that he should cultivate so the buddha told him that he should cultivate achievement in alertness achievement and weariness and keep good company and have an even life and achievement in alertness is to be skillful learn his work in his job marina is to be able to guard his wealth and his property good company is keeping company with good persons and even life is to be able to uh spend his money uh wisely and not not exceeding his income and not to be unduly elated or depressed and then the 4 channels what are flowing away of of wealth is looseness with women drinking gambling and having evil friends and the opposite leads to flowing in of great wealth and then 4 things lead to a person's advantage and happiness in the world to come just as before is the achievement in the faith having the faith in the buddha dharma and sangha virtue keeping the precepts charity that means being generous and practicing charity and the last 1 is wisdom that means knowing that things are impermanent that they arise and pass away and that basically means understanding the dhamma so these are the things that uh that will bring a person happiness in the world to come and today we come to this with
